---
title: "250905 Tobi Dfam repeats in subtelomere"
output: html_notebook
---


```{bash}
#working directory 
cd /gpfs/analyses/april/KARLSEDER/tobi/250905_dfam

#download Dfam currated motifs for hmmer
#Dfam release 3.9 (March 2025)
wget https://dfam.org/releases/current/families/Dfam-curated_only-1.hmm.gz

#pull latest dfam toolset from singularity
#nhmmscan HMMER 3.4 (Aug 2023); http://hmmer.org/
singularity pull dfam-tetools-latest.sif docker://dfam/tetools:latest
singularity run dfam-tetools-latest.sif

#within the singularity image
#modify the PATH to include the required tools nhmmer and trf are included
PATH=/opt/hmmer/bin:/opt/trf:$PATH

#prepare database for searching
gunzip Dfam-curated_only-1.hmm.gz
#press the uncompressed database
hmmpress Dfam-curated_only-1.hmm 

#move pressed db to it's own directory
mkdir Dfam-curated-only-1-hmm
mv Dfam-curated* Dfam-curated-only-1-hmm/

#convert Kat's telomere fastqs to fastq format
sed -n '1~4s/^@/>/p;2~4p' IMR90SVtelo_reads.fq > IMR90SVtelo_reads.fasta
sed -n '1~4s/^@/>/p;2~4p' IMR90ALTtelo_reads.fq > IMR90ALTtelo_reads.fasta

```

```{bash IMR90SV nhmmscan}
wc -l IMR90SVtelo_reads.fasta
#18076 telomere sequences in this file 

nhmmscan --noali --tblout IMR90SVtelo_tbl.out --cpu=56 Dfam-curated-only-1-hmm/Dfam-curated_only-1.hmm IMR90SVtelo_reads.fasta
```


```{bash IMR90ALT nhmmscan}
wc -l IMR90ALTtelo_reads.fasta
#8996 IMR90ALTtelo_reads.fasta

nhmmscan --noali --tblout IMR90ALTtelo_tbl.out --cpu=56 Dfam-curated-only-1-hmm/Dfam-curated_only-1.hmm IMR90ALTtelo_reads.fasta
```


```{bash awk file unique_readsv2.awk}
# AWK script to count the number of *distinct* reads that contain at least one occurrence
# of each tandem motif, and calculate the percentage of total unique reads they represent.
# This version skips the first 2 and last 10 lines of the input file.

# Array 'read_names': Stores $1 (Read Name) for all lines. Index is NR.
# Array 'motif_names': Stores $2 (Motif Name) for all lines. Index is NR.
# Array 'count': used for deduping (Motif, Read) pairs.
# Array 'all_reads': used to find the total number of unique reads (the denominator).

{
    # 1. Store the data for all lines in memory.
    # This prepares the data to be processed later, allowing us to reference the total line count (NR).
    read_names[NR] = $3
    motif_names[NR] = $1
}

# The END block runs after all input lines have been processed.
END {
    # Define the range of lines to process: from line 3 up to NR - 10 (inclusive).
    # Lines 1 and 2 are skipped. The last 10 lines (NR-9 through NR) are skipped.
    start_line = 3
    end_line = NR - 10

    # Safety check: ensure there are enough lines to process (i.e., NR >= 13)
    if (end_line < start_line) {
        # Print error message to stderr (>&2) and exit gracefully.
        print "Error: Input file has only " NR " lines. Need at least 13 lines to skip first 2 and last 10." | "cat >&2"
        exit 1
    }

    # Process only the lines within the required range [3, NR - 10]
    for (i = start_line; i <= end_line; i++) {
        read_name = read_names[i]
        motif_name = motif_names[i]

        # Deduplicate by (Motif, Read) pair for per-motif counting
        count[motif_name, read_name] = 1

        # Track all unique read names globally for the denominator calculation
        all_reads[read_name] = 1
    }

    # Determine the total number of unique reads found in the processed range.
    total_unique_reads = length(all_reads)

    # Re-aggregate the distinct read counts per motif from the 'count' array
    for (key in count) {
        # Split the composite key back into its two components: Motif and Read.
        # The key is "MotifSUBSEPRead"
        split(key, parts, SUBSEP)
        final_counts[parts[1]]++
    }

    # Print the final report header
    printf "%-25s %-15s %-10s\n", "TANDEM MOTIF", "DISTINCT READS", "PERCENT (%)"
    print "------------------------- --------------- ----------"

    # Iterate through the final sum array, calculate percentage, and print
    for (motif in final_counts) {
        motif_count = final_counts[motif]

        # Check if total_unique_reads is 0 to avoid division by zero
        if (total_unique_reads > 0) {
            # Calculate percentage: (Motif Reads / Total Reads) * 100
            percentage = (motif_count / total_unique_reads) * 100
        } else {
            percentage = 0.00
        }

        # Print the results, formatting the percentage to two decimal places
        printf "%-25s %15d %10.2f\n", motif, motif_count, percentage
    }

    print "------------------------- --------------- ----------"
    printf "PROCESSED LINE RANGE: %d through %d\n", start_line, end_line
    printf "TOTAL UNIQUE READS (in range): %d\n", total_unique_reads
}

```


```{bash IMR90ALT}
#IMR90ALT

#total number of telomere containing reads: 
wc -l IMR90ALTtelo_reads.fasta 
#8996 IMR90ALTtelo_reads.fasta -> 4498 reads

#total instances of motifs found 
wc -l IMR90ALTtelo_tbl.out
#9582168 IMR90ALTtelo_tbl.out

#total number of unique motifs found
awk 'NR > 2 {seen[$1]++} END {print length(seen)}' IMR90ALTtelo_tbl.out
#9325 unique targets

#create count table of 9325 unique motifs
awk 'NR > 2 { count[$1]++ } END { for (value in count) print value, count[value] }' IMR90ALTtelo_tbl.out  > IMR90ALTtelo_counts.txt
wc -l IMR90ALTtelo_counts.txt
#9325 IMR90ALTtelo_counts.txt

#get number of reads that contain at least one count of a given motif
awk -f unique_readsv2.awk IMR90ALTtelo_tbl.out > IMR90ALTtelo_motif_unique_read_counts.txt

```

```{bash IMR90SV }
#IMR90SV

#total number of telomere containing reads
wc -l IMR90SVtelo_reads.fasta 
#18076 IMR90SVtelo_reads.fasta -> 9038 telomere containing long reads

#total instances of motifs found
wc -l IMR90SVtelo_tbl.out
#17139630 IMR90SVtelo_tbl.out

#how many unique motifs found 
awk 'NR > 2 {seen[$1]++} END {print length(seen)}' IMR90SVtelo_tbl.out 
#7547 unique targets

#create count table of 7547 unique motifs
awk 'NR > 2 { count[$1]++ } END { for (value in count) print value, count[value] }' IMR90SVtelo_tbl.out  > IMR90SVtelo_counts.txt
wc -l IMR90SVtelo_counts.txt
#7547 IMR90SVtelo_counts.txt

#get number of reads that contain at least one count of a given motif
awk -f unique_readsv2.awk IMR90SVtelo_tbl.out > IMR90SVtelo_motif_unique_read_counts.txt

```



```{r make Venn diagrams}
setwd("/gpfs/analyses/april/KARLSEDER/tobi/250905_dfam")
library(VennDiagram)

#9325 elements
IMR90ALT <- read.delim("/gpfs/analyses/april/KARLSEDER/tobi/250905_dfam/IMR90ALTtelo_counts.txt",sep=" ",stringsAsFactors = F,header=F)
#7547 elements
IMRSV <- read.delim("/gpfs/analyses/april/KARLSEDER/tobi/250905_dfam/IMR90SVtelo_counts.txt",sep=" ",stringsAsFactors = F,header=F)

table(IMR90ALT$V1 %in% IMRSV$V1)
#FALSE  TRUE 
# 2780  6545 
#a lot overlap but some unique to both 

overlap <- list(IMR90ALT$V1, IMRSV$V1)

venn.diagram(x =overlap,category.names = c("IMR90ALT","IMR90SV"),
                 filename = "IMR90ALT_IMR90SV_DFAM_venn.png",
                 main = "Overlap of DFAM TRF Motifs",imagetype = 'png')

names(overlap) <- c("IMR90ALT","IMR90SV")
VennDiagram_obj <- get.venn.partitions(x = overlap)
simpleVennCSV(VennDiagram_obj, nam="IMR90ALT_IMR90SV_DFAM_venn")
```

```{r simpleVennCSV function}

simpleVennCSV <- function(VennDiagram_obj,nam){
  if (length(VennDiagram_obj$..count..) == 31 | length(VennDiagram_obj$..count..) == 7 | length(VennDiagram_obj$..count..) == 3 | length(VennDiagram_obj$..count..) == 15) {
    l <- lapply(X=1:length(VennDiagram_obj$..count..),FUN=function(x){
      if(length(as.data.frame(VennDiagram_obj$..values..[x])[,1]) > 0){
        setX <- data.frame(gene = as.data.frame(VennDiagram_obj$..values..[x])[,1],
                           set = rep(VennDiagram_obj$..set..[x],length(VennDiagram_obj$..values..[x])))} else{
                             setX <- data.frame(gene = "NA",
                                                set = rep(VennDiagram_obj$..set..[x],length(VennDiagram_obj$..values..[x])))}
      return(setX)
    })

    venn_overlap <- do.call("rbind", l)

    write.csv(x = venn_overlap, file = sprintf("%s_venn.csv",nam), quote = F, row.names = F)
    #openxlsx::write.xlsx(x = venn_overlap,file = sprintf("%s_venn.xlsx",nam),firstRow=T,keepNA=T)
  }else{
    print("Invalid Venn diagram object.")
  }
}
```
